exampleSentencesTranslated: The old man sits reading the newspaper in the park every morning.
When I was young, I often listened to the old man tell fairy tales.
I want to buy a small gift for the old man on his birthday.
notes_md:
**Ông già** means *old man*. It refers to an elderly male, usually someone clearly older.

It’s a noun. You can say **một ông già**, **hai ông già** — the classifier is **ông**.  
Depending on tone, it can sound neutral, affectionate, or a bit rude. For more respect, people often say **cụ** (very old man) or **người già** (elderly person, more neutral).  

Sometimes **ông già** is used jokingly or casually to refer to someone’s father, but tone matters a lot.
notes:
Ông già means old man. It refers to an elderly male, usually someone clearly older.

It’s a noun. You can say một ông già, hai ông già — the classifier is ông.  
Depending on tone, it can sound neutral, affectionate, or a bit rude. For more respect, people often say cụ (very old man) or người già (elderly person, more neutral).  

Sometimes ông già is used jokingly or casually to refer to someone’s father, but tone matters a lot.
exampleSentences: Ông già ngồi đọc báo ở công viên mỗi buổi sáng.
Khi còn nhỏ, em thường nghe ông già kể chuyện cổ tích.
Anh muốn mua một món quà nhỏ tặng cho ông già nhân dịp sinh nhật.

definitions.vi: Người đàn ông đã lớn tuổi.

example.vi: Ông già ấy rất hiền lành.
example.en: That old man is very kind.

translation.en: There is an old man walking.
Ask about the old man's health.
